text.title: embedding-generation Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for embedding-generation as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Switching embedding models mid-pipeline because "the new model ranks higher on MTEB".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Vector spaces are incompatible. Switching models invalidates all stored vectors — the entire collection must be re-indexed. This is an O(corpus size) operation that costs time and API budget.

failure-mode.description: Calling the embedding API once per text in a loop because "it's simpler".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: API round-trip latency is 0.5–2 seconds per call. Embedding 10,000 texts one-by-one takes hours; batching takes minutes. Batching is not optional.

failure-mode.description: Using MD5 for cache keys in production with billions of documents.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: MD5 collisions exist in practice. A collision silently returns the wrong embedding for a text. The probability becomes measurable at billion-document scale.

failure-mode.description: Using Euclidean distance on unnormalized vectors when cosine similarity was intended.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Euclidean distance conflates vector magnitude with direction. Cosine similarity requires normalization to measure directional agreement alone. Using the wrong metric silently produces wrong rankings.

failure-mode.description: Using Ollama embeddings in a per-text loop because "it's local".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Ollama has no batch endpoint; the loop is O(n) HTTP calls. For corpora of 10,000+ documents, this is impractically slow. Use SentenceTransformers instead for local batch embedding.

failure-mode.description: Embedding texts that exceed the model's token limit without validation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Some SDKs silently truncate overlong inputs; others raise an error. Either way, the tail of the text is lost, producing embeddings that do not represent the full content.

failure-mode.description: Initializing multiple SentenceTransformer models in the same process for "parallelism".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each model loads multi-GB of weights into RAM. Two instances of bge-large-en-v1.5 (1.3 GB each) exhaust a 16 GB GPU. Use a single model instance with higher batch_size instead.
